free_text: New experimental data, compilation and evaluation for
the natCu(a,x)66Ga, natCu(a,x)67Ga and natCu(a,x)65Zn
monitor reactions
,MGC 20E cyclotron in Debrecen
, CGR 560 cyclotron in Brussels
high purity Cu foils with 5-12 micron thickness
17 stacks containing each from 3 to 24 foils were
irradiated for 10 to 120 minutes with alpha particle
beams. Beam intensity was between 50 nA and
3 microA.
Charge integrated by Faraday-cup.  Nuclear
data were taken from E.Brwne and R.B.Firestone Table
of Radioactive Isotopes Ed.V.S.Shirley, Wiley 1986.
The mean energy in the foils were calculated using the
tables of H.H.Andersen, J.F.Ziegler, Hydrogen Stopping
Powers and Ranges in All Elements,Vol.3,Pergamon Press,
New York, 1977.
Data were taken from the Table 3. of the original paper
Approved by S. Takacs 29/11/96.
The error of the energy was calculated taking
into account the initial uncertainty the straggling of
the beam and the errors in the thickness of the targets
the estimated energy scale error was +-0.5 MeV at the
first foils and +-2.0 MeV at the last foil of a
long stack.
The error of the cross section data were
estimated by summing up the squares of the individual
relative errors and taking the square root of it.
Beam current             8%
Number of target nuclei  3%
Decay data               3%
Detector efficiency was  5%
Statisztical error and
Peak area determination  1-10%
Recoil losses            1%
The overall uncertainty reached  13%.
Recommended cross sections were calculated using
statistical fit over selected experimental data sets.
